steps.title: Identify the experimental finding
steps.content: The passage's key observation is that 73% of children chose the teacher who counted aloud as fairer, showing that the method of distributing rewards—counting aloud versus silently—influenced children's fairness perception.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice D directly states this: the way rewards are distributed may affect whether young children think a situation is fair. The other options either deny children's fairness understanding, focus on math instruction, or mischaracterize what children expect. That matches choice D — D is correct.
